title: The Way We Werent

jokes.text: Because I'm 'it.' Now whoever finishes first gets lemonade. BOTH: Yay! For me.
jokes.setup: Kids think they'll get lemonade for winning the raking contest
jokes.punchline: Bart reveals he's getting the lemonade regardless
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart manipulation with perfect timing and misdirection. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: You guys need your cootie shots. (BOTH CRYING OUT IN PAIN) Well, I hate this job, but I love the health plan.
jokes.setup: Girls pretend to give boys cootie shots
jokes.punchline: Girl delivers professional complaint about workplace benefits
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of adult workplace concerns with childhood games.

jokes.text: We'll find some other way to entertain our boy-crazy cousin who thinks you're cute. Oh, I wouldn't mind pushing that in the mud.
jokes.setup: Girl mentions boy-crazy cousin who likes Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's violent response to romantic interest
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart response to romance - violence instead of interest.

jokes.text: I hope you like the taste of ringworm medicine.
jokes.setup: Spin the bottle game about to result in kiss
jokes.punchline: Bart threatens disgusting medical consequence
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's perfectly in character for Bart avoiding romance.

jokes.text: You never had a chance to become my urine.
jokes.setup: Homer mourns his spilled beer
jokes.punchline: Overly specific and gross explanation of beer's destiny
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer line - treating beer with funeral solemnity while being disgusting.

jokes.text: I got the idea from a now discredited book on raising children.
jokes.setup: Marge explains Simpson Family Court concept
jokes.punchline: Casual admission the parenting method is debunked
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge deadpan delivery about questionable parenting choices.

jokes.text: Homer, I'll bet when you were 10, you were stealing beers, kissing girls, and tipping dinosaurs.
jokes.setup: Marge lists Homer's childhood misbehavior
jokes.punchline: Escalates from realistic to prehistoric absurdity
jokes.explanation: Great escalation joke that builds naturally to absurd conclusion.

jokes.text: It was years ago when I was 10, back in the '60s or '50s. Or maybe it was the early '70s.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to establish when his story took place
jokes.punchline: Can't pin down the decade, revealing his confusion about his own age
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer confusion about basic facts about his own life.

jokes.text: Underprivileged? You pretended we were poor just to get me into camp? Uh, yeah, pretended.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers deception about camp eligibility
jokes.punchline: Abe's admission they were actually poor
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Simpson family poverty with good economy. Abe's awkward admission works well.

jokes.text: Wow, a Negro. We prefer the term 'black.' So cool.
jokes.setup: Young Homer meets Carl with outdated terminology
jokes.punchline: Carl corrects him and Homer thinks the correction is cool
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate ignorance showing Homer's innocent cluelessness.

jokes.text: Moe's not really a counselor. His parents dropped him off here, like, two years ago and they never came back for him. I sleep under upside-down canoes.
jokes.setup: Introduction of Moe as junior counselor
jokes.punchline: Revelation he's actually an abandoned child living at camp
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark backstory for Moe that explains his character perfectly.

jokes.text: We got no water, no heat. We catch our own breakfast. Twelve kids died at our camp. We need the money to pay lawyers.
jokes.setup: Listing camp's poor conditions
jokes.punchline: Escalates to deadly consequences and legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from inconvenience to horror to bureaucracy.

jokes.text: Oh, here's one! It's called 'Stay out of my way.' Idiot.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about fun camp games
jokes.punchline: Director's 'game' is just aggressive dismissal
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection about camp activities. Good economy but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: A girl with teeth! Me like.
jokes.setup: Homer sees Marge for first time
jokes.punchline: Excited by basic dental hygiene in caveman speak
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's low standards and crude speech patterns perfectly.

jokes.text: That's 'cause I've eaten 16 pieces of pie.
jokes.setup: Marge comments Homer sounds sweet
jokes.punchline: Homer attributes sweetness to massive pie consumption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - literal interpretation with gluttonous specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, a switchblade! I see the switch, but where's the blade? D'oh! Found it.
jokes.setup: Moe gives Homer 'protection' for his date
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't understand how switchblade works and hurts himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity with physical comedy payoff.

jokes.text: Patches, scars, stumps. Everything but zits.
jokes.setup: Moe explains what girls find attractive
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly severe disfigurements with arbitrary exception
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation with absurd specificity about teenage priorities.

jokes.text: I guess it's row versus wade. And it's my right to choose!
jokes.setup: Discussing how to cross the lake
jokes.punchline: Pun on Roe v. Wade abortion case about crossing water
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting legal precedent to literal water crossing.

jokes.text: Only PG. Nothing R. Yarrr.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain explaining movie night rules
jokes.punchline: Pirate enforcing wholesome content standards
jokes.explanation: Character-specific irony - pirate as moral guardian. Good economy with signature 'Yarrr' catchphrase.

jokes.text: Until I met your mother, who made that girl look like a Godzilla made of garbage!
jokes.setup: Homer describes how pretty young Marge was
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and disgusting comparison to current Marge
jokes.explanation: Hilariously over-the-top comparison that's both romantic and insulting.

jokes.text: And if I'd known it was you, I never would have married you.
jokes.setup: Family thinks the childhood romance is sweet
jokes.punchline: Marge reveals it would have changed everything negatively
jokes.explanation: Dark twist that subverts romantic expectations perfectly.

jokes.text: Whee! Recess! Whee!
jokes.setup: Family court declares recess
jokes.punchline: Bart literally runs around celebrating like a school kid
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart behavior - treating adult situation like school. Character-appropriate physical comedy.

jokes.text: That's still one more than you. I've kissed three girls. (SOBBING) I'm so lame!
jokes.setup: Homer and Bart compare romantic experience
jokes.punchline: Bart's boast immediately collapses into insecurity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's bravado masking insecurity.

jokes.text: You make Jackie O look like a splay-legged milk-horse.
jokes.setup: Complimenting Marge's posture
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison mixing elegance with barnyard imagery
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd comparison that's both complimentary and grotesque.

jokes.text: I'd be proud if you grew up to be my husband's mistress.
jokes.setup: Teacher praising Marge's deportment
jokes.punchline: Aspires for student to become homewrecker
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny subversion of teacher encouragement with inappropriate goal.

jokes.text: I used to walk like this all the time until my tendons snapped. They heard it in Shelbyville.
jokes.setup: Explaining her refined walking technique
jokes.punchline: Escalates to grotesque injury audible in neighboring town
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from elegant to grotesque with geographic specificity.

jokes.text: I'm Elvis! Elvis Jagger. Elvis Jagger Abdul-Jabbar.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to give fake name quickly
jokes.punchline: Keeps adding celebrity names in increasingly absurd combination
jokes.explanation: Classic escalation showing Homer's panic and pop culture confusion.

jokes.text: One day as a straight-haired brunette. I feel so bad for you.
jokes.setup: Marge's hair ironing accident
jokes.punchline: Instructor's sarcastic sympathy for temporary change
jokes.explanation: Mild sarcasm about beauty standards. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: That's what Leslie Uggams does! BART: Leslie who? MARGE: Uggams!
jokes.setup: Marge references obscure 1970s celebrity for hair advice
jokes.punchline: Bart doesn't know who she's talking about, Marge insists on full name
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor showing Marge's dated cultural references.

jokes.text: We've heard the same story two times now. Whose side are we gonna hear next? The Sea Captain's?
jokes.setup: Lisa complaints about repetitive storytelling structure
jokes.punchline: Sea Captain appears and immediately leaves
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about storytelling structure with perfect timing.

jokes.text: I like Bobby Goldsboro, Gilbert O'Sullivan, Terry Jacks, Parliament, but not Funkadelic.
jokes.setup: Young Marge lists her musical preferences
jokes.punchline: Separates Parliament and Funkadelic despite being same band
jokes.explanation: Shows young Marge's selective taste and musical ignorance perfectly.

jokes.text: Is he making chicken noises?
jokes.setup: Homer nervously clucking during romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Marge's confused reaction to bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor - Homer's nervousness manifesting as animal noises. Marge's confusion is relatably awkward.

jokes.text: It felt like a cluster bomb wiping out a graveyard full of zombies.
jokes.setup: Homer describing how the kiss felt
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely violent and specific metaphor for romantic feeling
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd metaphor that's both romantic and violent in classic Homer style.

jokes.text: It was as satisfying as a million Hallmark cards with all the right sized envelopes.
jokes.setup: Describing the perfect kiss
jokes.punchline: Romantic metaphor about greeting card convenience
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and absurd romantic metaphor. Homer's unique perspective on romance with mundane office supply detail.

jokes.text: Face it, Lise. Men are dogs. The worse we treat you, the more you want us. That's not what dogs do. (LAUGHING) You said 'dog doo!'
jokes.setup: Bart makes cynical statement about relationships
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by accidental toilet humor
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart reverting to childish humor despite trying to sound mature.

jokes.text: But every time you smash a nerd with it, think of me. I will.
jokes.setup: Homer gives Marge romantic gift of heart-shaped rock
jokes.punchline: Suggests violent use and Marge agrees
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny romantic gesture with violence and mutual agreement.

jokes.text: But every time you smash a nerd with it, think of me.
jokes.setup: Homer's romantic gesture with the rock
jokes.punchline: Encouraging violence while maintaining romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark romantic sentiment - violence as expression of love. Perfect Homer logic and character.

jokes.text: Because the only way out is up a gentle slope.
jokes.setup: Counselor explains why no one escapes Fat Camp
jokes.punchline: The 'barrier' is barely an obstacle
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the 'inescapable' prison has minimal security due to inmates' unfitness.

jokes.text: Your boy bosoms tell a different tale.
jokes.setup: Homer claims he doesn't belong at Fat Camp
jokes.punchline: Counselor uses euphemistic term for Homer's chest
jokes.explanation: Funny euphemism for man-boobs with Shakespearean flair.

jokes.text: Hey, he didn't say nothing about this poison oak! Sweet, sweet poison.
jokes.setup: Counselor forbids eating grass
jokes.punchline: Homer finds loophole with toxic plant and enjoys it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding dangerous loophole and enjoying harmful consequences.

jokes.text: Yeah, you've got a date with a skinless chicken breast.
jokes.setup: Homer saying he has a date
jokes.punchline: Director's meal plan as romantic appointment
jokes.explanation: Functional food/romance pun. Decent structure but fairly predictable diet camp humor.

jokes.text: Hey, don't you try and prank me with a fake name! I will out your intestines and use them to make a lanyard!
jokes.setup: Homer calls asking for Elvis Jagger Abdul-Jabbar
jokes.punchline: Moe threatens grotesque violence ending in craft project
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from anger to creative violence with absurd crafting detail.

jokes.text: We know everything about boys except what they look like below the waist. I have some theories based on a G.I. Joe I bought.
jokes.setup: Girls claim to know all about boys
jokes.punchline: Admit ignorance based on inappropriate toy research
jokes.explanation: Awkward sexual ignorance with absurd research method.

jokes.text: We know everything about boys except what they look like below the waist. I have some theories based on a G.I. Joe I bought.
jokes.setup: Girls claiming to know about boys
jokes.punchline: Anatomical education from inappropriate source
jokes.explanation: Clever childhood innocence humor about anatomy education from toys. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: Because that's what I've been telling everybody. You're also pregnant.
jokes.setup: Girl asking if Marge is leaving because of a boy
jokes.punchline: Escalation to completely inappropriate rumor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of camp gossip to absurd extremes. Dark humor about childhood rumors.

jokes.text: We've got an emergency here. We need 10 cc's pronto. And by cc's, I mean cupcakes. MAN: I know what you mean, Bill. I've worked here longer than you.
jokes.setup: Medical emergency language at Fat Camp
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's about food, second doctor already knows the code
jokes.explanation: Good institutional humor about medical code for junk food.

jokes.text: I can't believe I went five minutes without one of these dream sticks.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain relieved after Homer escapes
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic name for cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny euphemism showing Sea Captain's questionable habits.

jokes.text: If they fight in front of us, we might get new bikes.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge having relationship tension
jokes.punchline: Bart's mercenary view of parental guilt
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart opportunism - seeing parental conflict as material opportunity. Character-specific and economical.

jokes.text: There was the gun I hid from you. The time I sued the church. Ruining Lisa's wedding in the future. Remember that?
jokes.setup: Homer lists worse things he's done than standing up Marge
jokes.punchline: References future episode that hasn't happened yet
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-reference to future episode breaking fourth wall casually.

jokes.text: Someday I'll write you back, Osama.
jokes.setup: Homer finds old letter from pen pal
jokes.punchline: Reveals pen pal is Osama bin Laden
jokes.explanation: Dark humor connecting Homer's innocent past to notorious terrorist.

jokes.text: I kept it right next to my IBM stocking.
jokes.setup: Homer shows he kept Marge's heart-shaped rock
jokes.punchline: Reveals he kept it with Christmas stocking from tech company
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate detail showing Homer's priorities and era.

jokes.text: As a reminder of the hurtful things men can do.
jokes.setup: Marge explains why she kept the heart rock
jokes.punchline: Kept romantic gift as symbol of male betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - turning romantic symbol into life lesson about disappointment.

tmdb_overview: Homer and Marge recall the story of their first kiss at a summer camp when they were children—an event which was not all happy, as Homer (unwillingly) betrayed Marge by standing her up on their second date.
